identity.name: Ikkousha Ramen Toronto
identity.address: 249 Queen St W, Toronto, ON M5V 1Z4, Canada
identity.location: 249 Queen St W, Toronto, ON M5V 1Z4, Canada

narrative.summary: Ramen-focused Toronto spot with strong local following and dependable bowls.

blocks.title: The Space
blocks.body: You're on Queen West between University and Spadina, where the street still feels a little gritty around the edges. Inside, it's narrow and functional—blonde wood counters, a few high stools facing the open kitchen, tables packed close enough that you hear conversations bleeding together. The air is thick with pork fat and garlic, steam rising from bowls that arrive in steady succession.

blocks.title: The Food
blocks.body: This is the tonkotsu-focused location—there's a chicken broth sister spot practically next door, so check the signage before you commit. The broth here is milky and rich, the kind that coats your spoon and doesn't apologize for it, built from pork bones simmered until they surrender everything. You'll find the classics: tonkotsu with chashu pork, spicy miso variations, and a spicy vegetarian bowl that draws its own loyal crowd. Bowls run around $15-18, with add-ons like extra noodles or marinated eggs available if you're still hungry.

blocks.title: Go When
blocks.body: This is for those brutal January nights when the wind off Lake Ontario makes your bones ache, or when you need something restorative after a long day and don't want to think too hard about it. Also excellent for a solo counter seat where you can watch the kitchen work and be done in thirty minutes flat.

blocks.title: The Vibe
blocks.body:
⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐ "With the frigid temperatures that Toronto is currently experiencing, Ikkousha's Ramen just felt like a nice warm blanket wrap by the fire. We had the Spicy Veg and the Fried chicken and both were excellent! The ingredients felt fresh. The broth very flavorful. The condiments greatly complimentary to the dishes.

The service was as needed, and the atmosphere was buzzing, a testament to the place. Highly recommend!"

⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐ "Very good ramen. I have been trying out many of the options in Toronto and this is one of the best. The unique part about this location as there are two Ikkoushas nearly next to each other. One focused on pork broth and the other on chicken so look closely on the signs outside of each location and pick your preference.

I love tonkotsu broth (pork) in general and this did not disappoint"

⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐ "Great everything. Once you enter you feel as the loud streets of Toronto disappear and you are teleported to the quiet streets of Japan. There's soft Japanese music playing in the background. The staff is super friendly. The menu is small 1 small sheet but you really don't need anymore. This was my second or third time trying ramen and I got the basic one with steamed rice and fried chicken. It was perfect. Came within 10 minutes of cooking and when I asked for a fork they polite gave me it. Very nice staff , very tasty food and great atmosphere. If I could give this a 11/10 or even 15/10 it would not give the place justice for how good they are."
